name: Terrain Authority
description: Terrain Authority is the foundational terrain generation tool of the Vale Terrain Suite. Rather than attempting to simulate real-world geology, it creates believable environmental forms through artistic composition and controlled procedural generation.
problem: Many terrain generators overwhelm developers with complex simulations and extensive parameter systems. Terrain Authority focuses instead on the desired result: compelling terrain foundations created quickly while preserving artistic control.
features: Parent and child regional terrain inheritance
Directional shelving for plateaus and stepped formations
Controlled ruggedness for natural visual breakup
workflowRole: Terrain Authority establishes the land itself. It creates the foundational terrain forms that every later stage of the VALE workflow organizes, traverses, expresses, grades, and paints.
releaseState: Package and consolidated release documentation are prepared; public sales activation remains pending.

name: Territory Authority
description: Territory Authority transforms large Unity terrains into standardized, interconnected territories designed for scalable world construction.
problem: Large terrains become increasingly difficult to organize and maintain. Territory Authority converts existing Unity terrains into standardized territory grids while preserving visual continuity.
features: Converts large terrains into standardized territory grids
Preserves terrain continuity through height reconstruction
Maintains visual cohesion across territory boundaries
Preserves the original terrain by disabling rather than destroying it
workflowRole: Territory Authority is the organizational foundation of the VALE workflow. It divides large terrains into standardized, connected territories and prepares the world for Path, Ribbon, Grade, and Paint Authority.
releaseState: Package and consolidated release documentation are prepared; public sales activation remains pending.

name: Path Authority
description: Path Authority is a reusable path authoring system designed to establish meaningful movement throughout a world.
problem: Many worldbuilding workflows bind movement and appearance together, making routes difficult to revise or extend. Path Authority separates route definition from visual expression.
features: Support for branching and extending existing routes
Designed for integration with Ribbon Authority
requirements: Recommended for use with Ribbon Authority
workflowRole: Path Authority establishes meaningful movement throughout the world. Terrain Authority creates the land, Territory Authority organizes it, and Path Authority defines how that world is traveled before Ribbon Authority gives those routes visible form.
releaseState: Package and consolidated release documentation are prepared; public sales activation remains pending.

name: Ribbon Authority
description: Ribbon Authority transforms Path Authority data into visible environmental features.
problem: Many worldbuilding tools combine route creation and visual representation into a single step, making routes difficult to revise and experimentation costly.
features: Generates visual features from existing Path Authority assets
Supports roads, rivers, bridges, trails, trenches, and other ribbon features
Supports animated materials and directional effects
workflowRole: Ribbon Authority provides the visible expression of movement established by Path Authority. It transforms reusable logical routes into roads, rivers, bridges, trails, trenches, and other environmental features without altering the original path data.
releaseState: Package and consolidated release documentation are prepared; public sales activation remains pending.

name: Grade Authority
description: Grade Authority is a terrain behavior authoring tool designed to establish meaningful environmental constraints through slope relationships rather than artificial restrictions.
problem: Modern games often rely on invisible barriers and inconsistent terrain sculpting to guide players. Grade Authority establishes believable environmental constraints through meaningful slope relationships.
features: Target grade authoring using meaningful slope values
Adjustable grade variance for natural results
Support for traversal design and environmental storytelling
workflowRole: Grade Authority teaches the environment what traversal possibilities exist. Following Terrain, Territory, Path, and Ribbon Authority, it establishes believable slopes and environmental constraints before Paint Authority completes the visual language.
releaseState: Package and consolidated release documentation are prepared; public sales activation remains pending.

name: Paint Authority
description: Paint Authority is a rule-based terrain texturing system designed to give environments visual voice through environmental relationships rather than repetitive manual painting.
problem: Manual terrain painting quickly becomes repetitive and inconsistent, especially across large worlds. Paint Authority applies textures through meaningful environmental rules.
workflowRole: Paint Authority is the final stage of the VALE terrain workflow. After the suite establishes land, territories, routes, visible features, and grades, Paint Authority gives the completed world its visual voice through rule-driven terrain texturing.
releaseState: Package and consolidated release documentation are prepared; public sales activation remains pending.
